nodes.headline: Telehealth sales needs warming layer between ad click and call to increase close rate
nodes.text:
# Sales Motion Building

> Telehealth sales needs warming layer between ad click and call to increase close rate

## Current State
- Google ads → straight to phone call
- No intermediate friction or qualification

## Framework
- Add: application/VSL/webinar layer
- Builds trust and pre-frames offer
- Qualifies hot leads only

## Claims
- Google ads for telehealth with straight-to-phone-call funnel is incomplete; needs warming layer (VSL/application/webinar) — [built_a_1_6m_practice_now_it_s_my_biggest_problem_G3NUEoTJ0Ec.txt]

## Connections
- Both concepts relate to sales and increase. _(connects to: Sales Motion Fundamentals)_
- Sales performance hinges on structured training and clear processes. _(connects to: Sales Motion Architecture)_
- Add warming layer between ad click and call to increase close rate; sales process is the constraint, not offer. _(connects to: Sales Process Redesign)_
- Sales motion optimization is foundation; telehealth needs warming layer to increase close rate. _(connects to: Sales Motion)_
- Close rates improve via better sales process; warming layer between ad and call directly improves closing mechanics. _(connects to: Close Rate Optimization)_
- Warming layer improves close rates; custom process with BANT qualification and credit card ready scales to 50-60%. _(connects to: Sales Process Design Closing Mechanics)_

---
_Source: built_a_1_6m_practice_now_it_s_my_biggest_problem_G3NUEoTJ0Ec.txt · 1 claim(s)_

nodes.headline: Free work is the fastest path to proof and real experience
nodes.text:
# Starting for Free

> Free work is the fastest path to proof and real experience

## How-To / Method
- Start for free
- Get 10 free clients
- Structure as free trial plus penalty
- Details on offer structure, timing, and outreach in 'Leads' book, page 51

## Claims
- If you don't have enough proof, start for free: work for free, get 10 free clients, make it a free trial plus penalty structure — [how_to_actually_become_successful_stop_copying_me_tDr3Ocu0VxE.txt]

## Connections
- Free work builds proof; paying client proof beats free, but free beats no proof. _(connects to: Testimonials As Currency)_
- Free work offers low-risk entry point to convert to paid after demonstrating value; free is fastest path to proof and real experience. _(connects to: Free Work Strategy)_
- Free generates 9x responses and works at scale (Facebook, Netflix); free work is fastest path to proof and experience. _(connects to: Free Offers)_

---
_Source: how_to_actually_become_successful_stop_copying_me_tDr3Ocu0VxE.txt · 1 claim(s)_

nodes.headline: Service businesses bottlenecked by talent supply; must invest in recruiting smarter people and robust training systems
nodes.text:
# Talent Recruitment and Training

> Service businesses bottlenecked by talent supply; must invest in recruiting smarter people and robust training systems

## Key Takeaway
- Service business constraint is always talent: supply of good people << demand; better people are harder to find and cost more
- Founder cannot expect to train someone in weekend to replicate 10-year skill mastery; this expectation leads to mediocre delivery and reputation damage
- Higher-tier roles (white-collar, professional services) require multi-year apprenticeship (analyst → associate → director) before independent work

## How-To
- Invest recruiting budget to attract highest-quality raw materials (smarter, higher-intelligence people) who ramp faster and deliver better
- Build robust training infrastructure and career paths so talent wants to stay; clear advancement (analyst → associate → director) creates retention and motivation
- Productize your knowledge and systematize delivery so training is teachable; this reduces founder bottleneck and compounds talent quality

## Examples
- Management consulting hiring (Ivy League backgrounds, entrepreneur experience) creates higher-quality delivery and better talent stickiness
- Career path clarity allows talent to see advancement opportunity; without it, hard-to-find people leave for competitors or start own firms
- Fitness coaching easier to hire (passionate people do it for free) but harder to retain customers; trades/white-collar harder to hire but customers stick because they don't want to learn

## Claims
- Service business talent investment priorities: recruit higher-quality raw materials (smarter, more intelligent people) and build strong employer brand to attract premium talent — [how_to_grow_any_business_faster_masterclass_qsXxckCbci0.txt]
Topic string 'Automation Prioritization' appears in 1 source(s).

nodes.title: 100M Playbook Lead Nurture
nodes.text: Alex Hormozi's Lead Nurture playbook outlines four pillars (Availability, Speed, Personalization, Volume) to increase show rates for scheduled appointments, derived from analysis of 4,000+ daily appointments in his ALAN software.
Founders do things 30% better than anyone else; founder is 68% of revenue, blocking scale to 80 agents.
Topic string 'Cash Flow Management' appears in 2 source(s).

nodes.title: 100M Offers
nodes.text: Guide to creating irresistible business offers by strategically combining pricing, value creation, guarantees, scarcity, urgency, and naming to build 'Grand Slam Offers' that generate massive profit without competing on price.

nodes.title: I'm so confused. Walk me through how you make money
nodes.text: An Easy Dwell founder does $3M revenue and $500K profit by buying properties, financing them to buyers via LLC structures, and holding 30-40 years of cash flow ($800/month per deal). The constraint is deal flow; he's only done 40 deals in 2 years and should master wholesaling or acquire wholesalers to increase volume.
Multi-location scaling requires owner time; split focus kills margins and growth.

nodes.headline: Structure incentivizes location performance and founder departure
nodes.text:
# General Manager Profit Share Structure

> Structure incentivizes location performance and founder departure

## Framework
- Base salary + profit share
- 5% base, +1% per year to cap at 10%
- KPI-based milestones

## Benchmarks
- On-target earnings (OTE) range for privately held manager:
- 75K if underperforming
- 100K on-target
- 120-130K if crushing

## Claims
- Hired GM starting in 2 weeks with 6-month shadow period to learn all operations before owner opens second location — [how_a_22_year_old_built_a_2_4m_pizza_shop_pobfyHIItag.txt]
- GM gets 5% base profit share, +1% per year capping at 10% with KPI hits - structure incentivizes location performance without over-commitment — [how_a_22_year_old_built_a_2_4m_pizza_shop_pobfyHIItag.txt]
- For privately held brick-and-mortar (not franchised), manager should have on-target earnings (OTE) around 100K if crushing, 120-130K if excellent, 75K if underperforming — [how_a_22_year_old_built_a_2_4m_pizza_shop_pobfyHIItag.txt]

## Connections
- 10-20% pool split creates ownership thinking; percentage fixed as pie grows aligns incentives. _(connects to: Profit Share Structure)_
- Structure incentivizes location performance; 10-20% pool distributes profits and aligns leader incentives. _(connects to: Profit Share Pool Structure)_

---
_Source: how_a_22_year_old_built_a_2_4m_pizza_shop_pobfyHIItag.txt · 3 claim(s)_

nodes.headline: Paid ads provide 3-5x short-term lift; organic content builds compounding long-term base; both required for balanced growth over 10-year horizon
nodes.text:
# Paid Ads vs. Organic Growth Foundation

> Paid ads provide 3-5x short-term lift; organic content builds compounding long-term base; both required for balanced growth over 10-year horizon

## Framework
- Organic: builds audience pyramid; paid ads reach same pyramid more efficiently
- Paid ads: short-term 3-5x lift but one-time effect
- Organic + paid: ads fill top of funnel, organic provides sustainable foundation

## Why It Works
- Organic audience becomes increasingly valuable over time (compound interest)
- Paid ads reach cold audience but economics deteriorate if organic foundation weak

## Implementation
- Short term: run paid ads for immediate growth
- Long term: invest in content creation, brand building, organic reach in parallel

## Claims
- Paid ads give 3-5x off baseline one-time; organic growth compounds long-term; ideal strategy is both in parallel: ads for short-term scale, content for long-term foundation — [helping_4_educational_business_owners_build_a_1m_business_in_BYpTRiRqS1Y.txt]
- Organic growth builds pyramid of audience; paid ads keeps same percentage conversion but lowers ad cost by reaching more cold audience; both needed for 10-year horizon — [helping_4_educational_business_owners_build_a_1m_business_in_BYpTRiRqS1Y.txt]

## Connections
- Organic content proves tactics; paid ads then scale proven winners across longer horizons. _(connects to: Content Driven Ad Funnel)_

---
_Source: helping_4_educational_business_owners_build_a_1m_business_in_BYpTRiRqS1Y.txt · 2 claim(s)_

nodes.title: Why Most Businesses Fail: No Standards
nodes.text: Businesses fail because owners don't establish clear standards and fail to model them; leadership by example is the foundation of any successful organization.

nodes.title: When I Showed Up with $0 in California
nodes.text: Founder's journey from homeless in California to gym owner: demonstrates how visceral desperation drives action; the suffering period lasts years, not seconds, and without true skin-in-the-game forced urgency, most won't execute.
